description_long: This is a robust, heavy-duty axe featuring a thick, rectangular metal head with a pointed end and a flat striking face. The head is made of gray, slightly textured metal, suggesting durability and strength for demanding tasks. The handle is straight, cylindrical, and wrapped in a black, ribbed grip for enhanced handling and control, with a visible brown section at the base that appears to be either wood or a synthetic material mimicking wood. The overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative elements, focusing on function over form. The axe is compact, likely intended for tasks such as demolition, masonry, or heavy-duty urban maintenance. There are no visible brand markings or engravings on the head or handle.
description: A compact, heavy-duty axe with a thick gray metal head and a black ribbed handle, featuring a pointed and flat striking face.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full rectangular metal head with a pointed end and the ribbed handle with a brown base.
description_view_1: Narrow edge-on view highlighting the thin profile of the blade and the cylindrical handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the pointed and flat faces of the head visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the flat back of the axe head and the straight alignment of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical handle with black ribbed rubber grip and a brown base, likely wood or synthetic, designed for secure handling.
attribute_blade_design: Thick rectangular metal head with one flat striking face and one pointed end, no beard, single edge, utilitarian design.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the head fitted onto the handle, no visible pins or modern composite features.
